title: CLI de aprobación: request, list, show, approve, deny y revoke
wave: Ola 3 — Seguridad operacional, aprobación humana y ejecución controlada

architectural_decision.reason: FUNC-SPRINT-29 exposes the existing approval domain through dependency-free local CLI commands. It introduces no external dependency, network call, provider, UI, destructive action or new storage backend. PolicyEngine binding is explicitly deferred to FUNC-SPRINT-30.

commands: python -m devpilot_core approval request --tool tests.run --action execute --subject pytest --reason "Validar cambios" --actor owner --json
python -m devpilot_core approval approve <approval_id> --actor owner --reason "Revisión OK" --json
python -m devpilot_core approval deny <approval_id> --actor owner --reason "Riesgo no mitigado" --json
python -m devpilot_core approval revoke <approval_id> --actor owner --reason "Ya no aplica" --json
pass_criteria: All approval CLI commands return CommandResult.
approval request creates a requested ApprovalRecord with scope and expiration.
approval list filters by status, tool and action.
approval show loads a single record by approval_id.
approval approve, deny and revoke require actor and reason.
Optional reports and local events are generated.
block_criteria: An approval is approved without actor or reason.
An expired approval allows future execution.
CLI output prints raw secrets or sensitive payloads.
Approval CLI is treated as automatic tool authorization before PolicyEngine binding.
risks: Actor is local/declarative; authentication and RBAC remain future work.
PolicyEngine binding is explicitly deferred to FUNC-SPRINT-30.
The CLI does not execute tests, patches, refactors or deploys.
Approval records are local SQLite evidence and require future retention/privacy controls.
next_sprint: FUNC-SPRINT-30 — Binding de aprobaciones con PolicyEngine y MIASI
